title: Stelechocarpus expansus
intro: Stelechocarpus expansus, commonly known as the Blue Mahoe, is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions, including wounds, skin infections, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Stelechocarpus expansus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
taxonomy: Stelechocarpus expansus is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. This plant is native to the tropical forests of New Guinea and nearby islands. Stelechocarpus expansus is also known as the 'Guanabana of New Guinea'. The classification of Stelechocarpus expansus places it within the order Magnoliales, which is a group of flowering plants known for their distinctive flowers and fruits. Further classification positions it as a member of the family Annonaceae, which is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
uses: Stelechocarpus expansus is known for its potential medicinal uses, particularly in traditional medicine. parts: Stelechocarpus expansus, commonly known as the Illawarra plum, is a plant native to Australia. The fruit of Stelechocarpus expansus is reported to be edible and has been used in traditional medicine by the indigenous people of Australia. side_effects: Stelechocarpus expansus is also known as the Mato or Monkey Fruit.
